description_long: This object is a rectangular, portable radio receiver with a classic design, featuring a prominent telescopic antenna extending from the top. The front face displays a wide frequency indicator panel with a grid and markings, likely for AM/FM tuning, above a large speaker grille. Two rotary knobs are visible on the lower right, presumably for tuning and volume control. The back side includes a battery compartment cover, ventilation slits, and various input/output ports. The casing appears to be made of hard black plastic with some metallic elements for the antenna and internal components. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for use in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or outdoor gatherings.
description: A rectangular, black portable radio receiver with a telescopic antenna, front frequency display, speaker grille, and rotary knobs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the frequency indicator panel, speaker grille, and two rotary knobs on the right.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the slim profile and the extended antenna, with knobs slightly protruding.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the battery compartment, ventilation slits, and input/output ports.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a plain, flat surface with minimal features.
